does: This report documents the comprehensive safety evaluation and red teaming process for OpenAI's o3-mini model. The work includes systematic safety testing across multiple risk categories, external adversarial testing conducted by red team researchers, and assessments using OpenAI's Preparedness Framework to evaluate frontier AI capabilities and risks. The report synthesizes findings from these evaluations to characterize the model's safety properties, identify potential harms or misuse vectors, and document the safety mitigations applied during development. The results provide transparency on both capabilities and limitations relevant to responsible deployment.
connects: This work builds on OpenAI's established practices in AI safety evaluation, extending prior system card efforts for models like GPT-4 and o1. It engages with broader literature on frontier AI evaluation frameworks, including evaluations for dual-use risks, chemical and biological threat assessment, and AI-enabled cyberattacks. The external red teaming approach reflects community standards developed through collaboration with academic researchers and safety organizations. The Preparedness Framework evaluation connects to emerging industry norms around evaluating advanced reasoning capabilities and model-specific risks, responding to increased scrutiny of large language model safety as deployment scales.
enables: This safety work supports responsible deployment of o3-mini by establishing baseline risk profiles and enabling informed decisions about appropriate use cases and guardrails. The evaluation methodology and findings can inform future safety assessments for similar reasoning-capable models, contributing to maturing practices in frontier AI evaluation. By transparently documenting safety properties and residual risks, the report enables external researchers, policymakers, and practitioners to build on this foundation when developing safety interventions or deployment strategies. The red teaming results and evaluation frameworks point toward developing more sophisticated detection methods for model misuse and increasingly rigorous safety standards as reasoning capabilities advance.
